Fung, Jin Q. Fung, Linda Fung And Franklin Fung
Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on regularly as scheduled for hearing plaintiff's request for Entry of Final Judgment for Partition and Sale of Real Property against defendants Jim K. Fung, Franklin Fung, Linda Fung and Jin Q. Fung.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Michael Shintaku, CSR #8251. Telephone: 408410-8040. Email: emailformike@hotmail.com.

Warren Woo, Esq., appearing for plaintiffs Amy Siu Ping Chan and Cindy Siu Fung Fung, as Successor Trustees of the Yam Kan and Lin Kwai Fung Trust, who were not present for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by defendants Jim K. Fung, Franklin Fung, Linda Fung or Jin Q. Fung.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Partition of Real Property was filed on January 9, 2015, and personally served on defendant Jim K. Fung on June 18, 2015.
Substitute service on defendant Linda Fung occurred on July 18, 2015, and was effectuated on July 30, 2015.
Substitute service on defendant Franklin Fung occurred on August 4, 2025, and was effectuated on August 15, 2015.
Amendment to plaintiff's complaint substituting Jin Q. Fung for DOE Number 1 was filed on October 23, 2015.
Substitute service on defendant Jin Q. Fung occurred on November 27, 2015 and was effectuated on December 10, 2015.
Default as to defendants Jim K. Fung and Franklin Fung was entered on September 29, 2015.
Default as to defendant Linda Fung was entered on October 9, 2015.
Default as to defendant Jin Q. Fung was entered on January 13, 2016.
Interlocutory Judgment was entered on November 10, 2016.
DOE defendants were dismissed on October 31, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiffs Amy Siu Ping Chan and Cindy Siu Fung Fung, and against the defaulted defendants Jim K. Fung, Franklin Fung, Linda Fung and Jin Q. Fung, as follows:

(1) The Default Interlocutory Judgment entered November 10, 2016 is hereby confirmed, adopted and incorporated into this Final Judgment;
(2) The Report of Sale of Real Property pursuant to the Interlocutory Judgment entered November 10, 2016 is hereby confirmed, adopted and incorporated into this Final Judgment;
(3) The Referee's allocation of the Anza Property sales proceeds, allocation of the attorney fees and costs to the parties, allocation of the referee fees and costs, and other credits and debits between the parties made by the Referee is hereby confirmed, adopted and incorporated into this Final Judgment;
(4) The costs of this partitiion action shall be apportioned between the parties; and
(5) Final Judgment for Partition by Sale and Sale of Real Property, for the subject property located at 4839 Anza Street, San Francisco, CA is entered in favor of plaintiffs Amy Siu Ping Chan and Cindy Siu Fung Fung, and against the defaulted defendants Jim K. Fung, Franklin Fung, Linda Fung and Jin Q. Fung.

Proposed Final Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

The Court informed Jim that from all the paperwork that the Court recieved it understands that during the divorce he gave his ex wife Jin Fung all the rights to the real estate, that there is no reason for his appearance today and stated that there was a lack of cooperation during this entire process from Jim and Jin. Jim stated that he knows, that the distribution amount for his ex wife does not reflect the cost of repairs and that he would like to get compenstated for that amount. Motion To Quash Service Of Summons, Set Aside Default And Default Interlocutory Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JIN FUNG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS, SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT

No appearances. The Court notes that no notice to contest the tentative ruling was given, and that an ex parte letter to the court from defendant was received and not considered. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1112(a)(1) and CRC 3.1110 (a). Also, moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that a motion to quash may be brought at this stage of the litigation, i.e. after default and interlocutory judgment have already been entered. To the extent this motion may be construed as a motion to vacate (in the absence of Notice of Motion or any authority cited in support of the motion to vacate) it is also DENIED because moving party failed to carry its burden under CCP 473(b), CCP 473(d), CCP 473.5 or for equitable relief. Finally, moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B in future. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Plaintiff Amy Chan, Cindy Fung'S Hearing For Default Interlocutory Judgment For Partition By Sale And Sale Of Real Property And To Take Account, And To Appoint Referee Against Defendants Jim K. Fung, Jin Q. Fung, Franklin Fung, And Linda Fung
Off Calendar Nov-10-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing for Default Interlocutory Judgment for Partition by Sale and sale of Real Property and to Take Account and to Appoint Referee against Defendants.
Dept. 514, Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: A. Mok. Proceedings reported by Denise Doucette, CSR#5963.

Warren Woo, Esq., appearing for and with Plaintiff AMY SIU PING CHAN, sworn and examined.
No appearance by or on behalf of Defendants JIM K. FUNG, FRANKLIN FUNG, LINDA FUNG, JIN Q. FUNG and all Persons Unknown Claiming any interest in the Property.
Default of Defendants has been duly entered.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs AMY SIU PING CHAN and CINDY SIU FUNG FUNG, current successor trustees of The Yam Kan and Lin Kwai Fung Trust, and against Defaulted Defendants JIM K. FUNG, FRANKLIN FUNG, LINDA FUNG, JIN Q. FUNG of the following described real property commonly known as: 4839 Anza Street, San Francisco, San Francisco County, State of California, Portion of outside land block no. 322, Assessor's Parcel Number: Block 1582, Lot 040I.

Plaintiffs are entitled to partition of the subject property under California CCP Sec 872.720. Sale of the subject property and division of the proceeds would be more equitable than physical division of the subject property.

Amy Harrington, Esq., is hereby appointed referee with authority to sell the property at public auction to the highest bidder for case except as may be provided by CCP sections 873.600-873.690 on notice duly given in the manner required for the sale of real property on execution, or by private sale in the event the referee shall determine that a private sale will be the most beneficial to all parties having interest therein and is hereby appointed referee to take and perform the accounting between the parties.

The referee after making the sale of the real property, is hereby directed to report to this court the referee's proceeding ins thereon, and, on confirmation of the sale of the real property by this court and the payment of the purchase price, and is hereby appointed referee to take and perform the accounting between the parties.

Plaintiffs, as trustees of the Yam Kan and Lin Kwai Fung Trust, are entitled to an accounting for financial issues related to the co-ownership of the subject property with Defendants, and each of them, for Defendants' occupancy and use of the subject property, and the costs of partition, including plaintiffs' attorney's fees and costs in this partition case.

No referee's bond is required at this time.
Judgment signed in open court.
